children.name: Holothuria
children.description: The largest and most diverse genus of sea cucumbers, containing over 100 species found in tropical and temperate waters worldwide. Many species are commercially harvested as trepang (bêche-de-mer).

children.name: Holothuria tubulosa
children.description: Common in the Mediterranean Sea on seagrass beds and soft sediments. Defends itself by expelling a mass of sticky white threads (Cuvierian tubules) that entangle predators.

children.name: Holothuria forskali
children.description: A large, dark-coloured sea cucumber of the eastern Atlantic and Mediterranean. Its body wall contains toxins (holothurins) used as a defence against predators.

children.name: Holothuria scabra
children.description: The most commercially valuable species of sea cucumber, heavily harvested throughout the Indo-Pacific for the Asian trepang trade. Overfishing has led to population declines and CITES consideration.

children.name: Actinopyga
children.description: A genus of large sea cucumbers characterised by five prominent teeth surrounding the anus. Widely harvested in the tropical Indo-Pacific for the trepang trade.

children.name: Actinopyga echinites
children.description: A heavily harvested trepang species of the Indo-Pacific, found on sandy bottoms and seagrass beds from East Africa to the western Pacific.

children.name: Actinopyga lecanora
children.description: Distinctive for its smooth, stone-like appearance and large size. Ranges across the Indo-Pacific from Madagascar to Fiji.

children.name: Bohadschia
children.description: A genus of tropical sea cucumbers with a soft, leathery body and striking colour patterns. Known for producing some of the most potent Cuvierian tubules among holothurians.

children.name: Bohadschia argus
children.description: Named for its distinctive brown and white spotted pattern resembling a tiger. Found on coral reefs and sandy areas of the Indo-Pacific.

children.name: Bohadschia vitiensis
children.description: A common species on Pacific coral reefs from Indonesia to Fiji. Harvested commercially but less prized than Holothuria scabra.

children.name: Pearsonothuria
children.description: A monotypic genus of tropical sea cucumber, notable for its distinctive black-and-yellow banded pattern in juveniles that changes to a mottled brown in adults.

children.name: Pearsonothuria graeffei
children.description: Found on Indo-Pacific coral reefs. Juveniles are strikingly patterned with black and yellow bands, resembling the toxic nudibranch Phyllidia, an example of Batesian mimicry.
